findings.description: Full card number and CVV are written to the logs in plaintext, a severe PCI-DSS violation exposing cardholder data.
CVV is accepted and passed through the processing path at all; PCI-DSS forbids storing or transmitting/handling CVV beyond the initial authorization, and it is logged here.
Transaction IDs are derived from the non-cryptographic, hashseed-dependent built-in hash(), making them predictable and potentially collidable rather than using a secure random/UUID.
Transaction id is a pure function of last-4 card digits and amount, so two distinct charges with the same last-4 and amount collide on the same tx id, breaking uniqueness.
monthly_revenue_report sums raw unit_price*quantity without applying discount or tax, so reported revenue does not match what charge_order actually charged (discounted subtotal plus tax).
charge_order never checks the order's current status, so an already-paid order can be charged again, causing double charges (no idempotency).
The final total is not rounded after applying tax, so compute_order_total returns a long-tailed float that can cause off-by-one-cent discrepancies when later converted via int(round(total*100)).
Monetary math uses binary floats (unit_price*quantity, *100) instead of Decimal, risking rounding errors in amounts charged and reported.
discount_pct is never validated, so a value >100 yields a negative line total (negative charge) and a negative value silently applies a surcharge.
monthly_revenue_report issues one order_items query per order inside the loop (N+1 query pattern) instead of a single aggregate/JOIN query.
charge does not validate card_number/cvv for emptiness or format; card_number[-4:] on an empty string silently yields an empty slice rather than erroring.
No explicit transaction/rollback handling around the UPDATE+commit; relies on implicit rollback at close, which is fragile for a financial mutation.
Using `order["discount_pct"] or 0.0` coerces any falsy discount value via truthiness rather than an explicit None check, a fragile money-handling idiom.
api_key is held as a plain instance attribute with no protection and is easily exposed via repr/logging/serialization of the gateway object.
A brand-new SQLite connection is opened and closed on every operation with no reuse/pooling, adding per-call overhead under load.
Return type is annotated as bare dict instead of a precise TypedDict/dataclass describing the report shape.
